messages.content: You write Sigil programs. Sigil is an S-expression language designed for minimal tokens. Programs may use (module name ...) wrapping or top-level script mode. CLI args: $0 is the first user arg as a string, #0 is the first as an int. (argv) returns the full CLI argv array; (split s d) splits a string. Use short aliases: len, str, fmt, split, join, sort, push, filter, map_arr, reduce, counter, sort_by, entries, find_all, regex_find, regex_match. (for i 0 n ...) is FIXED-STRIDE — mutating i inside the body raises an error. For variable-stride or skip loops use (while). Output ONLY raw Sigil code.
Take a multi-line text in arg0. For each MAXIMAL run of identical consecutive lines, print '   N line' (count right-aligned to width 4 then space then line).

(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set n (len lines))
(set i 0)
(while (lt i n)
  (set line (get lines i))
  (set j i)
  (while (and (lt j n) (eq (get lines j) line))
    (set j (add j 1)))
  (println (fmt "{:>4} {}" (sub j i) line))
  (set i j))

You write Sigil programs. Sigil is an S-expression language designed for minimal tokens. Programs may use (module name ...) wrapping or top-level script mode. CLI args: $0 is the first user arg as a string, #0 is the first as an int. (argv) returns the full CLI argv array; (split s d) splits a string. Use short aliases: len, str, fmt, split, join, sort, push, filter, map_arr, reduce, counter, sort_by, entries, find_all, regex_find, regex_match. (for i 0 n ...) is FIXED-STRIDE — mutating i inside the body raises an error. For variable-stride or skip loops use (while). Output ONLY raw Sigil code.
Take a multi-line text in arg0. Run-length encode each line as 'CH:N' for each maximal run of identical characters; print one encoded string per line.

(for-each line (split $0 "\n")
  (set n (len line))
  (set i 0)
  (set out [])
  (while (lt i n)
    (set ch (string_at line i))
    (set j i)
    (while (and (lt j n) (eq (string_at line j) ch))
      (set j (add j 1)))
    (push out (fmt "{}:{}" ch (sub j i)))
    (set i j))
  (println (join out " ")))

(for i 0 n ...) is FIXED-STRIDE — mutating i inside the body raises an error. For variable-stride or skip loops use (while). Output ONLY raw Sigil code.
Take an integer N as #0 and print every Nth integer from 0 (inclusive) up to 20 (exclusive), one per line. Example: N=3 prints 0,3,6,9,12,15,18.

(set step #0)
(set i 0)
(while (lt i 20)
  (println i)
  (set i (add i step)))

Take a space-separated string of tokens in arg0 (simple whitespace split, no quoting). Print 'NAME=N' where NAME is each distinct token and N is its count, sorted alphabetically by NAME, space-separated.

(set toks (filter (split $0 " ") (\t (ne t ""))))
(set counts {})
(for-each t toks
  (set counts (map_set counts t (add 1 (get_or counts t 0)))))
(set pairs (sort_by (entries counts) (\p (get p 0))))
(println (join (map_arr pairs (\p (fmt "{}={}" (get p 0) (get p 1)))) " "))

Take a space-separated string of words in arg0. Print each distinct word once, in the order of FIRST appearance, one per line.

(set seen {})
(for-each w (filter (split $0 " ") (\t (ne t "")))
  (if (not (has seen w))
    (do (set seen (map_set seen w true)) (println w))))

Take a space-separated string of integers in arg0. Print the sum of all the integers.
(println (sum (map_arr (filter (split $0 " ") (\t (ne t ""))) parse_int)))

Take a space-separated string of words in arg0. Print the count of distinct words.
(println (len (uniq (filter (split $0 " ") (\t (ne t ""))))))
